raw_text: ABAYOMI-COLE, A. B.—b. 1907; ed. Govt. Model Sch., Prince of Wales Sch., S.L., and Durham Univ.; M.O., S.L., 1944.

ABBOTT, A. W., C.M.G. (1956), C.B.E. (1949).—b. 1893; ed. Blundell's; entd. C.A. off. after compet. exam., 1912; mil. serv., 1917–19; lieut.; asst. hd., fin. dept., 1929; dep. hd., 1933; hd., 1938; sec., E.A. currency bd., 1930–38; estab. offr., 1948; sec., C.As., 1954.

ABBOTT, B., M.B.E.—b. 1913; ed. King Edward's Sch. and Tynemouth High Sch.; mil. serv., 1939–46, lt.-col.; cadet, Nig., 1946; admin. offr., cl. II, 1954; sec. to comsn. on the public service, Nig., 1954–55.

ABBOTT, B. A.—b. 1914; ed. King's Sch., King's Coll. and Univ. Coll., Auckland, N.Z., and Jesus Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1940–46; cadet, Nig., 1940; admin. offr., cl. II, 1954; i/c local govt. wing, inst. of admin., N. regn., 1954.

ABBOTT, M. J.—b. 1906; ed. King's Sch., Canterbury; barrister, Gray's Inn; mil. serv., 1941–45, lieut.; asst. crown solr., H.K., 1936; secon. to Ethiopian Govt. as pres., high ct., 1946; puisne judge, Nig., 1950; judge, 1955.

ABBOTT, S. H.—b. 1908; ed. Antigua Gram. Sch.; appt. agric. dept., Antigua, 1929; agric. asst., St. Kitts, 1939; farm manager, 1944; agric. offr., Virgin Is., 1946; Nevis, 1949; agric. supt., Antigua, 1955.
ABELL, Sir Anthony, K.C.M.G. (1952), C.M.G.—b. 1906; ed. Repton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; cadet, Nig., 1929; admin. offr., cl. II, 1946; cl. I, 1948; gov. and c.in-c., Sarawak, 1950.

ABERNETHY, J. S.—b. 1907; ed. Sedbergh Sch., Aberdeen Gram. Sch., Aberdeen and Edin. Univs.; interned, 1942–45; chartered co., N. Borneo; legal advr., comsnr. of lands, prot. of labr. (incl. inspr., schs.), 1936; food contrlr. (in addn.), 1941; sessions judge, B.M.A. (B. Borneo), 1946; comsnr., lands, 1947; sessions judge, 1948; res. mag., Tang., 1949; judge of high ct., 1951.

ABRAHAM, A. A.—b. 1912; ed. St. Stanislaus Coll., B. Guiana; cler. asst., B. Guiana, 1929; dist. offr., 1945; estab. offr., 1947; prin. asst. sec., 1953; ch. estab. offr., 1955, mem. publ. serv. comsn.
ABRAHAM, A. L., M.V.O. (1954), Q.P.M. (1957), C.P.M.—b. 1908; ed. Southend High Sch.; police, Pal., 1930; supt., Barb., 1938; Fiji, 1944; B. Hond., 1950; comsnr., Gib., 1953.
ABRAHAM, E. E.—b. 1900; ed. Royal Coll., Maur.; writer, treasy., Maur., 1920; clk., govt. depts., 1928–51; chief exec. offr., educ. dept., 1951; asst. sec., sec't, 1955.
ABRAHAMS, A. R.—b. 1908; ed. J'ca Coll.; apptd. J'ca, 1927; pensions offr., 1944; asst. sec., 1950; prin., 1952; perm. sec., min. of commns. and wks., 1954.

ABRAHAMS, H. A.—b. 1906; ed. Calabar High Sch., J'ca; asst., constab., J'ca, 1923; 2nd cl. clk., constab., 1933; 1st cl. clk., med. dept., 1939; examr., accts., audit dept., 1944; acctnt., dept. of agric., 1948; senr. acctnt., 1950; admin. sec., med. dept., 1952; man., govt. savings bank, 1955; prin. asst. sec., 1955.

ABRAHAMS, Rt. Hon. Sir Sidney, P.C. (1941), Kt. Bach. (1936), Q.C.—b. 1885; ed. Bedford Modern Sch. and Emmanuel Coll., Camb.; called to Bar, Middle Temp., June, 1909; hon. Bencher, 1948; Midland circuit; town mag., Zanz., 1915; cr. coun., Ken., 1915 (did not proceed); secon. Mespot. civ. admin., 1920; advoc.-gen. and registr. of companies, Baghdad, 1920; pres. ct. of first instance, ct. of sessions and ct. of criminal appl., Basrah, 1921; atty-gen., Zanz., 1922; Uga., 1925; G.C., 1928; ch. just., Uga., 1933; Tang., 1934; Ceylon, 1936-39; member of jud. comttee. of privy coun., 1941; jt. edr., Zanz. law rept., 1868-1918; sole edr., 1918-22; 2nd cls. Order of Brilliant Star of Zanz., 1924; addl. asst. leg. advr., C.O., 1939; sole comsnr. to enquire into Nyasaland land problems, 1946; edited (with A. Abrahams) Dagens Nyheter Stadian Edn., 1912; various contributions to journalism on track athletics.
ABRAMS, P. D.—b. 1921; ed. Kitale Sch. and Prince of Wales Sch., Ken., and Merton Coll., Oxford; admin. cadet, Ken., 1946; dist. offr., 1948.
ADAMES, P.—b. 1913; ed. Cranleigh Sch. and Oriel Coll., Oxford, Camb. Univ. and I.C.T.A.; agric. offr., S.L., 1940; prin. agric. offr., 1951; asst. dir., agric., W. Nig., 1956.
ADAMS, A. B.—b. 1899; ed. Wolmer's Sch., J'ca, London and Illinois Univ.; senr. master, tech. sch., San Fernando, Trin., 1954; prin., 1955.
ADAMS, H. L.—b. 1910; ed. Haileybury; mil. serv., 1939-45, lt.-col.; cadet, Ken., 1946; sec., comrce. and industry, 1949; econ. sec., E.A.H.C., 1954.

ADAMS, P. F., O.B.E., Medal for Merit (U.S.A.)—b. 1914; ed. Mitcham Secondary Sch. and Lond. Univ.; I.C.S., 1937-47; col. admin. serv., sec. to devel. coms., Tang., 1948; prin. asst. sec. (devel.), 1950; M.C.S., cl. IC, ag. contrlr., econ. division, 1953; mem., jt. working party, rubber industry, 1953-54; leader, Mal. reps., inter. rubber study group mtg., Colombo, 1954.
ADAMS, R. K.—b. 1911; asst. regisr., co-op. socs., S.L., 1939; Nig., 1946; S.L., 1950.
ADAMSON, A. R.—b. 1916; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trin., and Portsmouth Municipal Coll.; exec. engnr., Trin., 1947.

ADAMSON, E.—b. 1921; ed. Bootle Gram. Sch. and L'pool Univ.; mil. serv., 1943-46; junr. scien. offr., min. of aircraft prod., 1942; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Mal., 1946; senr. exec. engnr., P.W.D., S'pore, 1955.
ADAMSON, G. A. G., M.B.E.—b. 1906; mil. serv., 1940-41; game ranger, Ken., 1939; senr., 1955.

ADDIS, Sir Wm., K.B.E. (1955), C.M.G. (1948), Brill. Star (3rd cl.), Zanz. (1946).—b. 1901; ed. Rugby and Magdalene Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1939-45, lieut., Zanz. N.V.F.; asst. dist. comsnr., Zanz., 1924; secon. to D.O., 1933; dist. comsnr., Zanz., 1933; dist. offr., N. Rhod., 1936; dist. comsnr., Zanz., 1938; col. sec., Berm., 1945; ag. gov., Oct., 1945-May, 1946, and Mar.-Oct., 1949; dep. comsnr.-gen. (col. affrs.), S.E. Asia, 1950; gov. and c-in-c., Sey., 1953.

ADDISON, G. H.—b. 1911; ed. Kelsick Sch., Ambleside, R. Botanic Gdns., Kew, and Berlin Botanic Gdns.; mil. serv., 1941-46; curator, botanic gdns., S'pore, 1938; jt. author Malayan Orchid Hybrids (1956), etc.

ADDISON, J. S.—b. 1902; ed. Hillhead High Sch., Glasgow, and Edin. Univ.; mil. serv., 1941-42; asst. constr., forests, F.M.S., 1925; senr. asst., 1935; secon. Ken., 1942-44; civ. affrs. and forest dept., B.M.A. (Mal.), 1944-46; constr., forests, 1947.

ADDISON, W. R. L., M.B.E. (1956), M.C.—b. 1919; ed. Haileybury Coll. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1939-46, maj.; cadet, Uganda, 1948; A.D.O., 1950; asst. sec., E.A.H.C., 1952; clk., cent. leg. assembly, 1951-55.

ADEBO, S. O.—b. 1913; ed. St. Peter's Sch., Ake, Abeokuta Gram. Sch., and King's Coll., Lagos; cler. serv., Nig., 1933-42; admin. offr., 1942; asst. fin. sec., 1954; admin. offr., cl. I, W. Nig., 1955; jt. author Review of Nigerianisation Policy and Practice, 1953.
ADEDIPE, B. A.—b. 1912; ed. Catholic Training Coll., Ibadan, and St. Gregory's Coll., Lagos; barrister-at-law; cler. serv., Nig., 1933; inspr., police, 1945; crown counsel, 1950.

ADEMOLA, Sir A. A., Kt. (1957).—b. 1906; ed. King's Coll., Lagos, and Camb. Univ.; barrister-at-law; mag., Nig., 1939; puisne judge, 1949; ch. justice, W. regn., 1955; mem. of comsns. for revision of cts. legn. (1948) and to enq. into Enugu disturbances (1949), Nig.

ADIE, J. J.—b. 1913; ed. Kingsland Grange Prep. Sch., Shrewsbury Sch. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1940-42 (K.A.R. and O.E.T.A.); admin. offr., Zanz., 1937; asst. dist. comsnr., Pemba, 1938; Zanz., 1939; p.s. to Br. Res., 1939-40; asst. sec., 1942; senr. asst. sec. and p.s. to H.H. the Sultan, 1946; awarded Brill. Star of Zanz. (4th cl.), 1947; secon. C.O., 1949; sec., educ., labr. and lands, Ken., 1952; chief sec., Barb., 1957; author of various articles on Zanzibar.

AGG, A. D.—b. 1912; ed. S. A. Collegiate Sch., Cape Town; mil. serv., 1939-40, 2nd lieut.; arch. asst., P.W.D., N. Rhod., 1938; asst. archt., 1945; archt., 1949; asst. D.P.W. (buildings), Nyasa, 1955.
AGOSTINI, C.—b. 1914; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin.; control offr., Trin., 1941; dep. aerodrome supt., 1946; dir. of civil aviation, 1947.

AGOSTINI, J. E.—b. 1906; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin.; cler. appts., govt. depts., Trin., 1924-44; senr. clk., acctnt-gen.'s dept., 1945; police (immig.), 1947; prin. offr., 1949; asst. ch. immig. offr., 1952; dep. ch. immig. offr., 1954.

AGROTIS, M. S.—b. 1913; ed. American Univ., Beirut; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; registr., T.U., Cyp., 1941; off. receiver and registr., 1948. AHMED RASHID—b. 1918; ed. Zanz., Makerere Coll. and Royal (Dick) Vet. Coll., Edin.; asst. vet. offr., Zanz., 1942; vet. offr., 1951.

AHWAI, C.—b. 1920; ed. St. Joseph's Coll., H. Kong, and London Univ.; 2nd cl. clk., Trin., 1940; govt. shorthand writer, 1944; senr. clk., sec't, 1949; prin. offr. (sec. to public serv. comsn.), 1950; asst. sec., 1954.

AINLEY, Sir A. J., Kt. (1957), M.C.—b. 1906; ed. St. Bees Sch. and Oxford Univ.; barrister-at-law; mil. serv., 1939-45, maj.; dist. mag., G.C., 1935; cr. coun., 1936; puisne judge, Uga., 1946; ch. just., E. Nig., 1956.
AITKEN, I. H.—b. 1919; ed. Greenock High Sch. and St. Andrews Univ.; tax assessor, Ken., 1944; senr. immig. offr., 1948; dep. prin. immig. offr., 1952.

AITKEN, Maj. R.—b. 1910; ed. Gregg Comm. Coll., Ipswich; mil. serv., 1939-46, major; H.M. Prison Service, 1936; secon. supt., prisons, B. Hond., 1946; resig.; N.Z. Prisons Serv., 1947; re-appointed supt., prisons, B. Hond., 1949; dep. dir. of prisons, J'ca, 1951; supt., prisons, B. Guiana, 1955.
AKENHEAD, E., T.D.—b. 1913; ed. Rugby Sch.; solicitor; mil. serv., 1939-45; asst. land offr., Tang., 1946.

AKERS, R. L.—b. 1912; ed. Solihull Sch. and Birmingham Univ.; mil. serv., 1941-42, 1944-46, capt.; asst. engnr., drainage and irrig., F.M.S., 1936; senr. irrig. engnr., S. Leone, 1943; engnr., drainage and irrig., Mal., 1946; state engnr., gr. B., 1949; chief construc. engnr., drainage and irrig., Fed. of Mal., 1951; dep. dir., drainage and irrig., 1955.
AKIWUMI, A., M.B.E. (1951).—b. 1900; ed. Wesleyan Boys' Sch., Freetown, Queen's Coll., Taunton and Sch. of Metalliferous Mining, Cornwall; asst. inspr., mines, S.L., 1933 (regraded inspr., 1946).
ALAKIJA, O. B.—b. 1910; ed. C.M.S. Gram. Sch. and King's Coll., Lagos, Seaford Coll. and Guy's Hosp., London; house physician and surgeon, Nig., 1939; M.O., 1940; S.M.O. (admin.) 1952.
ALBURY, K. H. E.—b. 1920; ed. Govt. High Sch., Nassau; clk., public treasy., Bah., 1940; asst. audr., 1950.
ALCINDOR, F.—b. 1909; ed. Ideal High Sch., Trin.; barrister-at-law; apptd. Trin., 1933; clk., magistracy, 1940-49; senr. clk., 1949; assessor, inland rev., 1954; magistrate, 1954.

ALCOCK, R. N., C.P.M. (1952),—b. 1912; ed. Highbury Sch., King Edward VII Nautical Sch., London, and Hendon Police Coll.; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; police cadet, Nig., 1938; asst. supt., 1941; secon. S. Leone, 1943-44; senr. asst. supt., 1947; senr. supt., police, 1950; crown coun., N. Nig., 1955; publ. Police Instruction Book (3 edns.).

ALCOCK, W. G.—b. 1907; ed. Oxford Prep. Sch., Glasgow Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; solr. (Scotland); asst. lands offr., Tang., 1935; lands offr. and registr.gen., Nyasa., 1939; off. assignee, S'pore, 1948; comsnr. of estate duties in addition.
ALDERDICE, A. A.—b. 1912; ed. Sydney Gram. Sch., Univ. of Sydney, and London Hosp.; mil. serv., 1940-45, lt.-col.; M.O., gr. I, Trin., 1947; med. supt., Uga., 1950; senr. specialist, 1954.

ALDERTON, E. C.—b. 1908; ed. Sherborne Sch. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1939-45, lt.-col.; cadet, Nig., 1931; admin. offr., cl. II, 1950; cl. I, 1954; perm. sec., min. of internal affrs., E. regn., 1954; sec. to premier, 1956.

ALDRIDGE, A. S., O.B.E. (1955), M.C., Cross of Valour (Poland).—b. 1918; ed. Durham Sch.; mil. serv., 1939-46, maj.; admin. offr., Cyp., 1946; cl. II, 1951; cl. I, 1953; senr. admin. offr., 1954; secon., C.O., 1955; secon., under-sec. (internal security), Cyprus, 1955.
ALEONG, R. V. S.—b. 1911; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trin., Faraday Hse. Elec. Engrng. Coll., London; chief electric inspector, Trin., 1950.

ALEXANDER, A. L., Q.P.M. (1955), C.P.M. (1951).—b. 1907; ed. Wellingborough Sch.; Pal. police, 1931; sub-inspr., Trin., 1935; G.C., 1946; senr. asst. supt., 1948; asst. comsnr., 1949; dep. comsnr. (Ghana civil service), 1955.

ALEXANDER, D. A. R.—b. 1920; ed. St. Mary's Coll., St. Lucia, and Univ. Coll., London; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; dep. clk. of courts, J'ca, 1944; mag., Turks and Caicos Is., 1947; clk. of courts, J'ca, 1949; asst. legal dftsnn., 1951; legal dftsnn., 1955; W. Nig., 1957.

ALEXANDER, J. B.—b. 1912; ed. Tiffin Boys' Sch., Kingston-on-Thames, Imp. Coll. of Science and Tech., London Univ. and California Inst. of Tech., U.S.A.; interned, 1942-45; geologist, Nyasa., 1935; F.M.S., 1939; prin. geol., 1950.

ALEXANDER, J. L. N.—b. 1907; ed. Tavistock Gram. Sch. and Birmingham Univ.; mil. serv., 1940-45, capt. (desps. twice); water and drainage engnr., P.W.D., Uga., 1946; redesig. chief water and drainage engnr., 1953.

ALEXANDER, N. L.—b. 1907; ed. Clifton Coll., Bristol, and Oxford Univ.; interned, 1942-45; cadet, F.M.S., Chinese prot., 1931; cl. V, 1934, 3rd mag., Penang; D.O., Xmas I., 1936; asst. prot., Chinese Perak, 1937; cl. IV, 1938; cl. III, 1943; dep. comsnr., labr., Pahang, Kelantan, 1946; cl. II, dep. comsnr., labr., Penang, 1947; cl. IC, asst. state sec. (B) Perak, 1950; sec. Chinese affrs., Selangor, 1951; cl. IB, 1952; D.O., Kinta, sec., Chinese affrs., Perak, 1954.

ALEXANDER, W. G., M.B.E.—b. 1918; ed. Sedbergh Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1940-45, major; admin. cadet, G. & E. Is. Col., 1946; co-op. soc. offr., 1947; regist., co-op. soc., Maur., 1951; comsnr., coop. devel., Cyp., 1955; U.K. delegate, F.A.O./L.O. tech. mtg. on co-ops. in Asia and F.E., 1954; survey and Report on possibilities co-op. dev. and necessary co-op. legislation in G. & E. Is., 1947.
ALEXANDER, W. H.—b. 1907; ed. Wolmer's Sch., J'ca; barrister-at-law; appd. J'ca, 1927; dep. clk., 1939; clk. of courts, 1947; crown coun., 1950; senr., 1955; resdt. mag., 1955.
ALEXIS, F. C.—b. 1913; ed. Grenada Boys' Secondary Sch. and Royal Coll. of Surgeons, Edin.; dist. med. offr., St. V., 1935; supt. physician, col. hosp., Grenada, 1952; S.M.O., 1955.

ALFORD, R. E., C.M.G.—b. 1904; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford; nav. serv., 1940-45; cadet, Nig., 1928; asst. dist. offr., 1931; dist. offr. (clk., exec. and leg. couns.), 1938; senr. dist. offr., 1946; fin. sec., Zanz., 1947; ch. sec., 1952.
ALI, Ayub, O.B.E. (1956), M.B.E.—b. 1907; ed. Govt. Indian Sch., Nairobi; clk., sec't, Ken., 1929; asst. estab. offr., 1944; estab. offr., 1948; asst. sec. (estab.), 1955.
ALLAN, C. H.—b. 1921; ed. Hamilton High Sch., Canterbury Univ. Coll., N.Z., and Camb. Univ.; mil. serv., 1942-44; cadet, B.S.I.P., 1945; cl. B., 1949; cl. A., 1955.

ALLAN, J. A.—b. 1910; ed. Glasgow Univ. and W. of Scotland Agric. Coll.; lectr., Barb., 1936; tech. offr., Nat. Inst. of Agric. Botany, Camb., 1944; supt., agric., Cyprus, 1945; pasture research offr., Ken., 1947; agric. offr., N. Rhod., 1949.

ALLAN, W., C.B.E. (1954), O.B.E.—b. 1904; ed. North Kelvinside Sch., Glasgow Univ., London Univ. (Wye) and I.C.T.A.; asst. research offr., agric. dept., N. Rhod., 1928; asst. dir., agric. and entom., 1938; dir., agric., Maur., 1948; Cyp., 1954.

ALLAN, C. P.—b. 1901; ed. Glasgow Univ.; anaesthetist, K. Lumpur, 1927; M.O. and surgeon, gen. hosp., Johore Bahru, 1936; surgeon, gr. B, 1937; Perak, 1948; specialist surgeon, superscale gr. B, Selangor, 1951; gr. A, state surgeon, 1952.

ALLAN, C. P. S., M.V.O.—b. 1917; ed. Wanganui Collegiate Sch., N.Z., and Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1940-43, K.A.R.; admin. cadet, Uga., 1940; asst. dist. offr., 1942; asst. chief sec., 1953; perm. sec. for security and external relations, 1955.

ALLEN, D. J.—b. 1911; ed. Clive Hse. Sch., St. Edward's Sch., and Hertford Coll., Oxford Univ.; customs prob., F.M.S., 1933; asst. supt., customs and excise, 1936; customs offr., 1939; senr., 1939; contrlr., supplies, Aden, 1941-44; asst. compt., customs, Fed. of Mal., 1951.

ALLEN, D. M., M.B.E. (1949).—b. 1916; ed. Bloxham and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; cadet, admin., G.C., 1938; asst. dist. comsnr., 1939; dist. comsnr., 1945; admin. offr., cl. II, 1950; cl. I, 1952; perm. sec. (Ghana civil service), 1956.

ALLEN, E. C., O.B.E.—b. 1902; ed. Bradford Commercial Sch. and Sch. of Accountancy; clk., sec't, and reporter, leg. co., Nyasa., 1925; asst. treas., Tang., 1930; senr., 1940; secon. as treas., ch. of customs, British Som., 1940; fin. sec. and collr. of inc. tax, Basuto., 1945.

ALLEN, E. F.—b. 1914; ed. The Leys Sch. and Trinity Hall, Camb. and I.C.T.A.; mil. serv., 1941-45; agric. offr., F.M.S., 1937; B.M.A. (Mal.), 1945; agric. offr., 1946; agric. offr. (research), 1950; senr. agric. offr., 1950; (senr. agronomist), 1952; att. F.A.O. confs. Ceylon and Tokyo; cacao conf., London.
ALLEN, H. P.—b. 1903; ed. Cornwall Coll., J'ca, and L'pool Univ.; barrister-at-law; clk. of courts, J'ca, 1936; resdt. mag., 1939.

ALLEN, J. G. C., C.M.G. (1956).—b. 1904; ed. Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, Ecole Superieure de Commerce, Lausanne, and Munich Univ.; mil. serv., 1940-43, maj.; cadet, Nig., 1926; senr. dist offr., 1945; cl. I, 1947; admin. offr., staff gr., 1953; E. regn., 1955; Br. comsnr., Anglo-French Cameroons Boundary Comsnr., 1937-39; Nig. govt. rep. to Free French, Duala, 1940; i/c. mil. infell. and censorship, Nig. 1940-41; liaison offr. to Gen. Leclerc, 1942; mem., res. min's staff Accra, 1943; labour offr., 1943-44; off. "goodwill" visit, Algeria and Morocco, 1949.
ALLEN, J. W. H.—b. 1913; ed. Regent St. Poly. and Downing Coll., Camb.; asst. audr., Nig., 1936; C.A.D., 1938; Fiji, 1943; senr. audr., Mal., 1948; dep. dir., fed. audit., Nig., 1954.

ALLEN, J. W. T.—b. 1904; ed. Westminster and St. John's Coll., Oxford; supt., educ., T.T., 1929; asst. dist. offr., 1937; dist. offr., 1942; political offr., Aden, 1947; dep. Br. agt., W. Aden Prot., 1948; D.O., Tang., 1953; senr., 1954.

ALLEN, R. L.—b. 1914; ed. Coombe Down C. of E. Sch.; Br. P.O. 1942-47; Sudan govt. posts and tels. dept., 1945; E.A. posts and tels. dept., techn., gr. I, 1947; insptg. techn., 1949; supv. techn., 1954.

ALLEN, T. H., Q.P.M. (1956).—b. 1908; ed. Brockley Secondary Sch., London; interned, 1942-45; prob. inspr., police, S.S., 1929; inspr., 1932; senr. 1937; prob. asst supt., police, S'pore, 1946; asst. supt., 1947; chief police offr., Brunei, 1948; supt., S'pore, 1952; supt., radio police, 1954.

ALLEYNE, K. H. C.—b. 1918; ed. Dominica Gram. Sch., Univ. Coll., London, and Queen's Coll., Oxford (Devonshire 2nd course); barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; registr., Dominica, 1943; mag., St. L., 1944; cr. atty., 1949; St. V., 1952; atty.-gen., Wind. Is., 1956; author of Memoir of constitutional development of St. Lucia, 1950.
ALLIN, P. W. M.—b. 1906; ed. Hilton Coll., Natal, and Rhodes Univ., Capetown; land survr., N. Rhod., 1930; senr. survr., 1945; dep. dir., surv. and land, 1951.
ALLISON, J. A.—b. 1915; ed. St. John's Coll., Jo'burg, and Witwatersrand Univ.; mil. serv., 1939-46, capt.; cadet, Nig., 1938; D.O., 1946; Bech. Prot., 1950; high comsnr's. office, 1950-51.
ALLISON, P. A.—b. 1907; ed. Caterham Sch. and St. Catharine's Coll., Camb.; asst. consvr., forests, Nig., 1931; senr. asst., 1949; consvr., 1954.
ALLPRESS, P. L.—b. 1921; ed. Beaumont Coll. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1941-42, 2nd lieut.; cadet, Nig., 1942; admin. offr., cl. II, E. Nig., 1955.

ALLSEBROOK, G. P.—b. 1918; ed. Stowe Sch., Kent Sch., Conn., U.S.A. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law; mil. serv., 1939-43, capt.; cadet, Tang., 1940; dist. offr., 1942; secon., adminr., Virgin Is., 1956.
ALP, W. C.—b. 1907; ed. Christ's Hosp. and London Univ.; mil. serv., 1940-45; archt., P.W.D., Ken., 1948.
ALTORFER, A.—b. 1915; ed. Calday Grange Gram. Sch.; mil. serv. 1940-46; prin. local govt. inspr., Ken., 1950.
ALVARES, R. E.—b. 1909; ed. St. Joseph's Coll., H.K. and H.K. Univ.; asst. pathologist, H.K., 1939; pathologist, 1951.
AMAH, D. J.—b. 1916; ed. King's Coll., Lagos, and St. Andrews Univ.; M.O., Nig., 1944; E. Nig.
AMAR, G. S.—b. 1912; ed. Punjab Univ.; asst. master, Ken., 1942; educ. offr. (Asian), 1947; senr., 1949.

AMATO-GAUCI, F. E., M.B.E. (mil.) (1945)—b. 1917; ed. St. John Academy and Royal Univ. of Malta; mil. serv., 1939-45, maj.; civ. serv. (admin.), Malta, 1936; prin. offr., 1948; dir. of labr., 1949; dir., emig., labr. and soc. welf., 1956; (also ex off. registr., trade unions, and chmn., workers' comp. bd., labr. bd., youth advsy. comtee., port workers' joint coun., port workers' bd.).

AMBIAVAGAR, V.—b. 1907; ed. Methodist Boys' Sch., K. Lumpur and Raffles Instn., and Raffles Coll., S'pore; pupil teacher, S'pore, 1925; cl. I. teacher, 1928; graduate teacher, 1931; spec. teacher (English), gr. I, 1950; prin., Beatty secondary sch., 1954; dep. sec. "B", min. of educ., 1955.

AMBROSE, J. W. D.—b. 1909; ed. Free Sch., Penang and Oxford Univ.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; asst. offr. assignee, S'pore, 1936; police mag. and asst. dist. judge, Malacca, 1940; registr., superior ct., 1945; dep. publ. prosecutor, N. YOUNG, W. M.—b. 1917; ed. Wellington Coll. and Camb. Univ.; interned, 1942–45; cadet, M.C.S., 1939; cl. V, 1942 (retros.); cl. IV, 1946; asst. comsnr., labr., Mal., 1948; cl. III, 1950; dep. comsnr., labr., Fed. of Mal., 1955; ag. sec., external def., 1956.

YOUNGER, W. M., M.B.E. (Mil.) (1941).—b. 1906; ed. Boys' High Sch., Pretoria and Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria; mil. serv., 1939–45, maj.; survey dept., N. Rhod., 1930; survr., 1939; senr., 1946; ch. survr., 1955.

YOUNGLAO, L. P.—b. 1901; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin., Univ. Coll., Dublin, L.S.H.T.M. and Johns Hopkins Univ., U.S.A.; M.O., Dominica, 1930; S.M.O. and M.O.H., 1945; S.M.O., B. Hond., 1948; M.O., Trin., 1950; D.D.M.S., 1955; sanitary serv., 1955; preventive, 1957.
YUEN, Kwok Huen—b. 1903; ed. Diocesan Boys' Sch. and H.K. Univ.; master, Queen's Coll., 1927; inspr., vernac. schs., 1948; senr. educ. offr., 1952; H.K. advr. to U.K. del., UNESCO, Bangkok, 1951.
ZAMMIT, C. G.—b. 1911; ed. Lyceum, Malta and Univ. of Lond. Inst. of Archaeol.; asst. custodian, archaeol., 1930; custodian, 1934; dir., museum, Malta, 1955.
ZAMMIT, J.—b. 1898; ed. St. Aloysius Coll. and Royal Univ. of Malta; sub. clk., 1919; civ. serv. (admin.), Malta, 1922; dir., printing office, 1946.

ZANNETIDES, C.—b. 1898; Licencié en Droit, Academie d'Aix, Univ. d'Aix, Marseilles; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; off. receiver in bankruptcy, 1940; dist. judge, Cyp., 1941; pres., dist. ct., 1949; puisne judge, 1955.

ZANS, V. A.—b. 1904; ed. Riga Univ.; govt. geologist, (later dir., geol. surveys) J'ca, 1949; publ. Economic Geology and Mineral Resources of J'ca (Bull. No. 1 Geol. Surv. Dept., 1951); On Karst Hydrology in J'ca (Assn. Internationale d'Hydrologie Scientifique—Bruxelles 1951—Extr. Tome II, 1951); Manganese Deposits of Marshall's Hall, J'ca and Bauxite Resources of J'ca and their Development (Col. Geol. Min. Res., Vol. 3, Nos. 2 and 4, 1952).

ZEKIA, M.—b. 1903; ed. Univ. of Istanbul; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; dist. judge, Cyp., 1940; pres., dist. ct., 1947; puisne judge, 1952; comsnr. of enq. for Lefkonico incidents, 1945; chmn., comtee. on Turkish affrs., 1948 and of admin. comtee. under Detention of Persons law, 1955.

ZENON, L. E.—b. 1903; Licencié en Droit, Univ. of Paris; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; dist. judge, Cyp., 1942; pres., dist. ct., 1952. PARLIAMENTARY AND NON-PARLIAMENTARY PAPERS OF COLONIAL INTEREST PUBLISHED DURING 1956 Arranged in three parts: (a) periodicals, (b) by subject, (c) by territories.
